title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas argues that current persona-evaluation benchmarks systematically fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where culturally dominant representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing character simulations to produce convincing but anachronistic outputs. He proposes a framework shifting from cognitive simulation to epistemic simulation with historian-in-the-loop evaluation, validated through the pre-registered Prism Experiment testing historical figure reasoning across documented temporal moments.
